message:
test(core): extend proptest coverage for BudgetEnforcer (ft-2p9cb.1.1.3)

AARSP A1 Verify: comprehensive property-based test coverage for the
latency stages module including the BudgetEnforcer runtime.

New property tests (9 added, 34 total):
- enforcer_no_overflow_within_budget: 1μs never triggers overflow
- enforcer_always_overflow_above_p999: 2× p999 always triggers overflow
- enforcer_observation_count_consistent: total equals recorded count
- enforcer_overflow_bounded: overflows ≤ observations
- enforcer_snapshot_coverage: snapshot has all pipeline stages
- enforcer_snapshot_serde: JSON roundtrip preserves all fields
- enforcer_log_overflow_only: within-budget observations don't log
- enforcer_mitigation_monotonic_severity: p95 ≤ p99 ≤ p999 severity
- enforcer_build_run_total_consistent: run total equals sum of stages

Closes ft-2p9cb.1.1 (parent) and all subtasks (.1.1.1, .1.1.2, .1.1.3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2p87e","title":"fix(frankenterm-color-types): gate std-dependent tests behind feature flag for no_std compilation","description":"68 compilation errors in frankenterm-color-types when building without std feature. Tests added by other agents call methods like contrast_ratio, lighten, to_hsla, complement, triad etc. that are behind #[cfg(feature = "std")] but the test functions themselves lack the gate. Fix: add #[cfg(feature = "std")] to all tests that call std-gated methods.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralFinch","created_at":"2026-02-14T03:08:59.752626Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:11:56.348581Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T03:11:56.348566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb","title":"[EPIC] Alien Artifact Reactivity Supremacy Program","description":"## Background
FrankenTerm already has strong performance and reliability initiatives. This program is the radical unification layer that turns those initiatives into a mathematically grounded, verifiable, user-perceived reactivity advantage.

## Vision
Build the most reactive, performant, and robust terminal runtime in the world by combining:
1. Formal latency contracts and deterministic scheduling.
2. Zero-copy data movement and hardware-aware execution.
3. Bayesian and sequential-statistical control loops with explicit risk accounting.
4. Model-checked safety invariants and replay-isomorphism evidence.
5. Fault containment and user-facing explainability that preserve trust under stress.

## Program Hypotheses
1. User-perceived responsiveness is dominated by tight control of critical-path input latency, not average throughput.
2. Determinism + replayability are multiplicative force multipliers for optimization speed and reliability.
3. Formal controls (expected-loss + e-processes) outperform static thresholds in non-stationary swarm workloads.
4. Engineering velocity improves when every optimization ships with isomorphism proofs and artifact-grade evidence.

## Non-Negotiable Constraints
- No feature loss relative to existing behavior.
- Fail closed on safety-critical paths.
- Every task includes unit + integration/e2e coverage and structured logs/artifacts.
- All optimization claims must be benchmarked and reproducible.

## Success Criteria
1. Program delivers quantifiable p95/p99 latency, stability, and MTTR improvements across representative workloads.
2. Optimization and reliability claims are enforced by CI gates (benchmark, replay, chaos, and proof artifacts).
3. Operator and agent workflows are faster and safer with clear explainability when safeguards trigger.
4. Future teams can execute from Beads alone without needing external markdown planning docs.

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Every track produces deterministic fixtures, golden outputs, and evidence bundles.
2. End-to-end scenario matrix includes nominal, degraded, failure, recovery, and soak paths.
3. Structured logs must include: timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code or error_code.
4. CI must fail on missing artifacts, proof-gate regressions, or budget violations.

## User Impact
1. Lower tail-latency and faster time-to-feedback under heavy agent swarm load.
2. Fewer terminal stalls, fewer ambiguous failures, faster root cause analysis.
3. Higher operator trust due to deterministic behavior and transparent safeguards.

## Future-Self Notes
Treat this epic as an execution contract: if a task does not improve measurable user outcomes with reproducible evidence, it is not complete.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.457565Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:13:13.904048Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","architecture","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","performance","program","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":1233,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Program-level execution rationale (alien-artifact + extreme-optimization):\n1. Control objective is user-perceived reactivity under adversarial load, not synthetic throughput.\n2. We optimize around deterministic tail-latency control: budget algebra + lane isolation + bounded adaptation + formal/replay proofs.\n3. Every parent task now has a mandatory three-stage subtask chain: Spec -> Impl -> Verify, with explicit dependency edges.\n4. Verification stage is where comprehensive unit/property/integration/e2e/chaos/soak scripts and structured evidence artifacts are required.\n5. Logging contract across all tracks is fixed: timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code.\n6. Priority policy revised: articulation-point chain promoted to P0 to maximize unblock rate and user-facing responsiveness gains early.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:19:52Z"},{"id":1263,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Canonicalization decision (2026-02-23):\n\nTo eliminate excessive overlap while preserving full capability scope, the reactivity planning surface is consolidated onto ft-2p9cb and its descendants.\n\nSuperseded open trees selected for merge retirement:\n- ft-189at*\n- ft-ocplf*\n- ft-1u90p.(8|9|10)*\n- ft-e34d9.11*\n\nNo-loss guardrail:\n- Imported/unique obligations from these trees are tracked by ft-2p9cb.7 (+ subtask chain ft-2p9cb.7.1 -> .7.2 -> .7.3).\n- Cross-epic traceability explicitly links transaction semantics (ft-1i2ge.8), replay/counterfactual (ft-og6q6), asupersync deep runtime integration (ft-e34d9.10), and trauma/loop resilience (ft-so7qh).\n\nValidation doctrine remains mandatory: comprehensive unit tests, deterministic e2e scripts, and structured logs/artifacts for every imported requirement.","created_at":"2026-02-23T01:39:43Z"},{"id":1270,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Continuation pass summary (2026-02-23): lint hardening + in-progress dedupe audit.

1) Lint hardening results
- Starting state: `br lint` reported 63 open-bead template warnings.
- Missing sections were limited to:
  - `## Acceptance Criteria` (60 beads)
  - `## Success Criteria` (3 beads)
- Applied description hardening via `br update` across all flagged IDs with explicit scope-preserving criteria and mandatory unit/integration/e2e + structured logging obligations.
- Final state: `br lint --json` => total=0, issues=0.

2) In-progress dedupe audit
- Audited all `in_progress` beads (48 total).
- `bv --robot-suggest --suggest-type duplicate` found no high-confidence duplicate pair touching any `in_progress` bead.
- Normalized-title collision check across `in_progress` set found 0 exact duplicates.
- Manual deep review of the highest-risk overlap cluster (asupersync migration lane: `ft-brc7d.*`, `ft-e34d9.10.5.*`, `ft-p48pw`, `ft-2h5wv`, `ft-1m7nk`, `ft-124z4`, `ft-181uk`) confirmed complementary layering (parent-child/blocking decomposition), not true duplicate intent.

3) Merge decision
- No additional in-progress merges were executed in this pass because no pair met the bar for safe duplicate retirement without capability/detail loss.
- Existing dedupe posture remains governed by prior canonicalization work (`ft-2p9cb.7*`) plus explicit dependency edges in migration clusters.
","created_at":"2026-02-23T19:13:13Z"},{"id":444,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Rationale: This epic overlays existing performance/reliability work with a single mathematically principled operating model. It is intentionally proof-heavy to prevent optimization theater and to keep future changes auditable.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04Z"},{"id":445,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution doctrine: profile first, optimize one lever at a time, prove behavior isomorphic, then expand.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1","title":"[AARSP][Track A] Latency Physics Contract and Budget Enforcement","description":"## Track Goal
Establish a formal latency physics contract so responsiveness targets are explicit, measurable, and enforceable.

## Why This Matters
Without stage-level budget algebra, teams optimize locally and regress globally.

## Deliverables
- Latency budget decomposition model.
- Runtime budget enforcement and adaptive allocation.
- Operator-facing knobs and policy-safe defaults.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Every critical pipeline stage has explicit latency budgets and instrumentation.
2. Budget violations trigger deterministic mitigation paths with clear reason codes.
3. Contract is covered by unit and e2e tests with artifact-backed evidence.

## Test and Observability Plan
Unit tests for budget math and edge handling; e2e scripts for overload and recovery; structured latency traces and decision logs.

## User Impact
Operators get predictable response times and clear explanations when the system degrades gracefully.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.642999Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.732451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","slo","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":446,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Future-self: if this track is weak, all downstream optimization tracks drift into heuristic tuning with unclear outcomes.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","title":"[AARSP][A1] Latency stage decomposition and budget algebra","description":"## Background
Reactive systems fail when latency budgeting is implicit.

## Objective
Define formal stage decomposition and budget algebra for input-to-visible-response path.

## Scope
- Enumerate stages and interfaces.
- Define p50/p95/p99/p999 targets per stage.
- Define composition rules and slack policies.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Budget model covers all critical stages and edge paths.
2. Composition math is validated by unit tests and adversarial cases.
3. E2E scenarios verify aggregate and per-stage budget accounting.
4. Structured logs include per-stage budget deltas and overflow reasons.

## User Impact
Predictable response behavior and faster diagnosis of latency hotspots.","notes":"BronzeGrove progress 2026-02-23: implemented deterministic latency contract + budget algebra in crates/frankenterm-core/src/latency_model.rs (QuantileBudgetMs, StageSlackPolicy, LatencyPathContract, evaluate_latency_budget, canonical input_to_visible_response_contract_v1) with adversarial unit coverage. Validation: rch-executed cargo fmt --check PASS; cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings FAIL due existing repo-wide clippy debt outside this bead. latency_model filtered tests: 78 passed.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.787922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:34:07.472726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency-math","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1214,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.1.1.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.1.1.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.1.1.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.1.1.2 -> ft-2p9cb.1.1.1 and ft-2p9cb.1.1.3 -> ft-2p9cb.1.1.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:41Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.1","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Spec] ft-2p9cb.1.1 :: Contracts, invariants, benchmark and test matrix","description":"## Objective\nTurn parent bead into a deterministic execution contract with measurable outcomes.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Boundary map for code paths, interfaces, and failure domains touched by the parent task.\n2. Explicit invariants and impossible-state definitions with reason-code taxonomy.\n3. Benchmark contract: workload classes, p50/p95/p99 targets, pass/fail criteria, and overhead budgets.\n4. Verification matrix: unit, property, integration, e2e, chaos, and soak scenarios including nominal/degraded/recovery cases.\n5. Structured logging contract (required fields): timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Spec is precise enough that two engineers implement the same behavior.\n2. Every success/failure mode has explicit test ownership.\n3. Benchmark and test matrix include edge-case and adversarial conditions.\n4. User-facing impact is mapped to measurable metrics and guardrails.\n\n## User Impact\nReduces ambiguity, prevents regressions, and accelerates reliable delivery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.643839Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:06:37.546843Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T20:06:37.546830Z","close_reason":"Spec complete: latency_stages.rs with 50 unit tests + proptest_latency_stages.rs with 25 property tests.  {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Impl] ft-2p9cb.1.1 :: Deterministic implementation and guardrails","description":"## Objective\nImplement the parent capability with deterministic semantics, bounded resource behavior, and explicit fallback policy.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Core implementation for planned fast/slow paths with explicit guardrails.\n2. Failure-handling and degradation ladder with stable reason codes.\n3. Performance-sensitive path optimizations validated against the benchmark contract.\n4. Operator-visible controls and diagnostics required by parent scope.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Behavior matches the subtask spec contract and parent acceptance criteria.\n2. No feature loss and no silent behavior drift.\n3. Deterministic behavior under fixed input/seed/config for replay scenarios.\n4. Structured logs are emitted at all critical decisions and failure transitions.\n\n## User Impact\nDelivers real responsiveness/reliability gains without sacrificing predictability.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.719434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:12:00.429644Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T20:12:00.429618Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: BudgetEnforcer with sliding-window percentile estimation, overflow detection, mitigation recommendations, structured logging, and diagnostic snapshots. 69 unit tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","implementation","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.719434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.876599Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","title":"[AARSP][A2] Budget instrumentation and correlation propagation","description":"## Objective
Implement nanosecond-grade instrumentation points and correlation propagation for budget accounting.

## Scope
- Timer probes at all stage boundaries.
- Correlation id propagation across async boundaries.
- Minimal overhead logging strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Instrumentation adds bounded overhead with benchmark evidence.
2. Correlation chain is complete for end-to-end traces.
3. Unit + e2e tests validate correctness and missing-probe failure detection.
4. Logs/artifacts are CI-exported for every performance test run.

## User Impact
Operators can pinpoint why responsiveness changed instead of guessing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.860Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:19:45.458987Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency","observability","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1191,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.1.2.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.1.2.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.1.2.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.1.2.2 -> ft-2p9cb.1.2.1 and ft-2p9cb.1.2.3 -> ft-2p9cb.1.2.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:24Z"}]}
